description: A designed graphic mimicking a Chicago Tribune online news article header on a light gray background. At top is the Chicago Tribune masthead in its signature blue blackletter (Old English) font, with a thin rule beneath it and a breadcrumb 'NEWS > CRIME AND PUBLIC SAFETY' in gray sans-serif. Below, in the Tribune's black serif headline font, is the original headline 'Chicago police arrest [Rogers Park man] in Loyola slaying' — but DHS has editorially altered it: the words 'Rogers Park man' are struck through with a hand-drawn red line, and red italic bold capitalized text 'TWICE RELEASED CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN' has been inserted above/over that line. The effect reads: 'Chicago police arrest TWICE RELEASED CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN [~~Rogers Park man~~] in Loyola slaying.' The propaganda technique is a 'fixed the headline for you' edit attacking a mainstream newspaper for an alleged euphemistic 'omission' (calling the suspect a 'Rogers Park man' rather than an illegal alien), reinforcing the immigrant-crime narrative. Companion tweet text accuses the Tribune of a 'BLATANT OMISSION' regarding the murder of Sheridan Gorman. Target audience: supporters receptive to media-bias and immigrant-crime framing.
notable_text: Chicago Tribune | NEWS > CRIME AND PUBLIC SAFETY | Chicago police arrest TWICE RELEASED CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN [Rogers Park man, struck through] in Loyola slaying
